meta.name: FoMO Fear of Missing Out Scale
meta.description: The FoMO Scale is a 10-item self-report measure assessing the pervasive apprehension that others might be having rewarding experiences from which one is absent. It captures fears and worries related to missing out on social experiences and the desire to stay continually connected with what others are doing.
meta.author: Przybylski et al. (2013)
meta.reference: Przybylski AK, Murayama K, DeHaan CR, Gladwell V. Motivational, emotional, and behavioral correlates of fear of missing out. Comput Human Behav. 2013;29(4):1841-1848.
meta.instruction: Below are a series of statements about your everyday experiences. Please indicate how true each statement is of your general experience.

commonChoices.label: Not at all true of me

questions.text: I fear others have more rewarding experiences than me
I fear my friends have more rewarding experiences than me
I get worried when I find out my friends are having fun without me
I get anxious when I don't know what my friends are up to
It is important that I understand my friends' "in jokes"
Sometimes, I wonder if I spend too much time keeping up with what is going on
It bothers me when I miss an opportunity to meet up with friends
When I have a good time it is important for me to share the details online
When I miss out on a planned get-together it bothers me
When I go on vacation, I continue to keep tabs on what my friends are doing

ranges.label: Low FoMO
ranges.description: Your score suggests a low level of fear of missing out. You are generally comfortable with not always being involved in social activities and do not feel compelled to constantly stay connected.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your healthy relationship with social media and social activities
Keep practicing being present in the moment
Your balanced approach to social engagement is a strength

ranges.label: Moderate FoMO
ranges.description: Your score indicates a moderate level of fear of missing out. You sometimes worry about missing social experiences but it does not significantly disrupt your daily life.
suggestions: Practice mindfulness to focus on present experiences
Set intentional limits on social media checking
Remind yourself that social media often shows a highlight reel, not reality
Invest in deeper connections with a few close friends rather than trying to keep up with everyone

ranges.label: High FoMO
ranges.description: Your score suggests a high level of fear of missing out. You frequently worry about what others are doing and may feel anxious about being left out of social experiences.
suggestions: Consider reducing social media use, especially passive scrolling
Practice gratitude for your own experiences and relationships
Engage in activities that bring you personal fulfillment independent of others
Challenge the belief that others are always having better experiences
Consider speaking with a counselor about managing social anxiety

ranges.label: Very High FoMO
ranges.description: Your score indicates a very high level of fear of missing out. This pervasive worry about missing social experiences may be significantly affecting your well-being and daily functioning.
suggestions: Consider a structured digital detox or significant reduction in social media use
Seek professional support to address underlying anxiety and self-esteem concerns
Practice self-compassion and challenge social comparison habits
Focus on building meaningful in-person connections
Develop personal interests and hobbies that provide intrinsic satisfaction
